bibliography:
Matias, Rogério, 2018, Cálculo Financeiro, Teoria e Prática, 6ª edição, Escolar Editora, Lisboa
	Matias, Rogério; Silva, Ilídio, 2008, Cálculo Financeiro, Exercícios Resolvidos e Explicações, Escolar Editora, Lisboa
methodology: Dynamic interaction of students with digital platforms.
objectives:
Teaching financial calculus techniques to prepare the students with skills to execute financial calculus exercises, namely:

- Know and understand the basic concepts regarding financial calculus

- Gain know-how on discounting and capitalization calculations

- Learn to calculate an annuity

- Learn to calculate loans¿ reimbursements

- Learn to analyse the companies¿ financing options

- Know to prepare spreadsheets to compute financial calculus exercises
organicUnit: ECATI - School of Communication, Architecture, Arts and Information Technologies
programme:
1. Basic concepts

2. Capitalization models

2.1. Simple interest rate

2.2. Compound interest rate

3. Equivalence of capitals

3.1. Value equation

3.2. Single capital term

3.3. Average due date

3.4. Average interest rate

4. Equivalence of interest rates

4.1. Nominal and efective rates

4.2. Equivalent rates

4.3. Instantaneous capitalization rate

4.4. TAEG (all inclusive rate)

5. Annuities

5.1. Definition and types

5.2. Constant terms

6. Loans

6.1. Classic loans

6.1.1. Basic concepts

6.1.2. Types regarding principal reimbursement and interest payments

6.2. Bond loans

6.2.1. Basic concepts

6.2.2. Types
presentation:
The course unit is part of the general training plan in the Management Informatics degree, seeking to provide students with the main concepts of Financial Calculation in order to support decision

making, critical topic in the business context.
avaliacao:
